title: Artisan

infobox.value: (from [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *artisan*, [Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *artigiano*) is a [skilled craft worker](/wiki/Skilled_worker "Skilled worker") who makes or creates material objects partly or entirely by [hand](/wiki/Handicraft "Handicraft"). These objects may be [functional](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/functional "wikt:functional") or strictly [decorative](/wiki/Beauty "Beauty"), for example [furniture](/wiki/Furniture "Furniture"), [decorative art](/wiki/Decorative_art "Decorative art"), [sculpture](/wiki/Sculpture "Sculpture"), [clothing](/wiki/Clothing "Clothing"), [food items](/wiki/Artisanal_food "Artisanal food"), household items, and [tools](/wiki/Tools "Tools") and mechanisms such as the handmade clockwork [movement](/wiki/Mechanical_watch "Mechanical watch") of a [watchmaker](/wiki/Watchmaker "Watchmaker"). Artisans practice a [craft](/wiki/Craft "Craft") and may through experience and [aptitude](/wiki/Aptitude "Aptitude") reach the expressive levels of an [artist](/wiki/Artist "Artist").
Peppas, Lynn (2005). [*Life in Ancient Greece*](https://archive.org/details/lifeinancientgre0000pepp). Crabtree P

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Skilled craft worker who makes or creates things by hand

This article is about the type of worker. For the vocal harmony group, see [Artisan (group)](/wiki/Artisan_(group) "Artisan (group)"). For the defunct media company, see [Artisan Entertainment](/wiki/Artisan_Entertainment "Artisan Entertainment"). For the publisher, see [Artisan Books](/wiki/Workman_Publishing_Company "Workman Publishing Company").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bc/Bessie_Potter_Vonnoh_in_her_studio_by_Jessie_Tarbox_Beals%2C_c._1905_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Bessie_Potter_Vonnoh_in_her_studio_by_Jessie_Tarbox_Beals%2C_c._1905_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bessie_Potter_Vonnoh_in_her_studio_by_Jessie_Tarbox_Beals,_c._1905_(cropped).jpg)

American sculptor [Bessie Potter Vonnoh](/wiki/Bessie_Potter_Vonnoh "Bessie Potter Vonnoh") in her studio

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/85/Traditional_Bagh_hand_block_print_master_craftsman-artisan-artist_Mohammed_Bilal_Khatri%2C_Madhya_Pradesh%2C_India.jpg/250px-Traditional_Bagh_hand_block_print_master_craftsman-artisan-artist_Mohammed_Bilal_Khatri%2C_Madhya_Pradesh%2C_India.jpg)](/wiki/File:Traditional_Bagh_hand_block_print_master_craftsman-artisan-artist_Mohammed_Bilal_Khatri,_Madhya_Pradesh,_India.jpg)

[Traditional hand block print](/wiki/Bagh_print "Bagh print") artisan in [India](/wiki/India "India")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8c/3_tourist_helping_artist_blacksmith_in_finland.JPG/250px-3_tourist_helping_artist_blacksmith_in_finland.JPG)](/wiki/File:3_tourist_helping_artist_blacksmith_in_finland.JPG)

An artist blacksmith and a striker working as one

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/95/Bali_0701a.jpg/250px-Bali_0701a.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bali_0701a.jpg)

A wood carver in [Bali](/wiki/Bali "Bali")

An **artisan** (from [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *artisan*, [Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *artigiano*) is a [skilled craft worker](/wiki/Skilled_worker "Skilled worker") who makes or creates material objects partly or entirely by [hand](/wiki/Handicraft "Handicraft"). These objects may be [functional](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/functional "wikt:functional") or strictly [decorative](/wiki/Beauty "Beauty"), for example [furniture](/wiki/Furniture "Furniture"), [decorative art](/wiki/Decorative_art "Decorative art"), [sculpture](/wiki/Sculpture "Sculpture"), [clothing](/wiki/Clothing "Clothing"), [food items](/wiki/Artisanal_food "Artisanal food"), household items, and [tools](/wiki/Tools "Tools") and mechanisms such as the handmade clockwork [movement](/wiki/Mechanical_watch "Mechanical watch") of a [watchmaker](/wiki/Watchmaker "Watchmaker"). Artisans practice a [craft](/wiki/Craft "Craft") and may through experience and [aptitude](/wiki/Aptitude "Aptitude") reach the expressive levels of an [artist](/wiki/Artist "Artist").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Artisan&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The adjective "artisanal" is often used in describing hand-processing in contrast to an industrial process, such as in the phrase *[artisanal mining](/wiki/Artisanal_mining "Artisanal mining")*. Thus, "artisanal" is sometimes used in [marketing](/wiki/Marketing "Marketing") and [advertising](/wiki/Advertising "Advertising") as a [buzz word](/wiki/Buzz_word "Buzz word") to describe or imply some relation with the crafting of handmade food products, such as [bread](/wiki/Bread "Bread"), [beverages](/wiki/Beverages "Beverages"), [cheese](/wiki/Artisanal_cheese "Artisanal cheese") or [textiles](/wiki/Textile "Textile"). Many of these have traditionally been handmade, [rural](/wiki/Rural "Rural") or [pastoral](/wiki/Pastoral "Pastoral") goods but are also now commonly made on a larger scale with automated [mechanization](/wiki/Mechanization "Mechanization") in [factories](/wiki/Factories "Factories") and other industrial areas.

Artisans were the dominant producers of primary [products](/wiki/Product_(business) "Product (business)") before the [Industrial Revolution](/wiki/Industrial_Revolution "Industrial Revolution").

In [ancient Greece](/wiki/Ancient_Greece "Ancient Greece"), artisans were drawn to [agoras](/wiki/Agora "Agora") and often built workshops nearby.[[1]](#cite_note-liag-1)

### Medieval artisans

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Artisan&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Medieval artisans")]

During the [Middle Ages](/wiki/Middle_Ages "Middle Ages"), the term "artisan" was applied to those who made things or provided services. It did not apply to unskilled [manual labourers](/wiki/Manual_labour "Manual labour"). Artisans were divided into two distinct groups: those who operated their own businesses and those who did not. The former were called [masters](/wiki/Master_craftsman "Master craftsman"), while the latter were the [journeymen](/wiki/Journeyman "Journeyman") and [apprentices](/wiki/Apprenticeship "Apprenticeship").

One misunderstanding many people have about this social group is that they picture them as "workers" in the modern sense: employed by someone. The most influential group among the artisans were the masters, the business owners. The owners enjoyed a high [social status](/wiki/Social_status "Social status") in their communities,[[2]](#cite_note-2) and organised into [guilds](/wiki/Guild "Guild") in towns and cities.

## Shokunin

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Artisan&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Shokunin")]

Shokunin ([職人](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%81%B7%E4%BA%BA#日本における職人 "ja:職人")) is a Japanese word for "artisan" or "craftsman", which also implies a [pride](/wiki/Pride "Pride") in one's own work. In the words of shokunin Tashio Odate:

> Shokunin means not only having technical skill, but also implies an attitude and social consciousness... a social obligation to work his best for the general welfare of the people, [an] obligation both material and spiritual.[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)

Traditionally, shokunin honoured their tools of trade at New Year's – the sharpened and taken-care of tools would be placed in a [tokonoma](/wiki/Tokonoma "Tokonoma") (a container or box still found in Japanese houses and shops), and two rice cakes and a tangerine (on top of rice paper) were placed on top of each toolbox, to honour the tools and express gratitude for performing their task.[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)
